./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5750:handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:7:**Why this doc exists** (A8e7 structural rewrite, 2026-04-26): rounds 1–6 of the Phase A → B audit produced a recurring documentary CHALLENGE pattern. Diagnosis: `A8_EXIT_PACKET` was conflating two opposite temporal modes (stable-snapshot artifact + append-only chronology) in one document, so every fix to "current state" generated new staleness in "round-N retrospective" sections. Splitting the two modes into two documents removes the category error.

The chronological history of audit rounds + their in-cycle fix bundles lives in the **companion** document `handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md` (append-only). Reviewers needing closure-of-prior-finding context read the history doc; reviewers verifying current-state correctness against Phase B prerequisites read THIS packet.

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:51:**Document split (A8e7)**: this packet is the **stable current-state snapshot** of Phase A at exit. The chronological history of audit rounds 1–N + their in-cycle fix bundles lives in the **companion** document `handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md` (append-only). Past audit transcripts are at `handover/audits/{CODEX,GEMINI}_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26{,_R2,_R3,_R4,_R5,_R6,...}.md`. Reviewers needing the full chronology read the history doc; reviewers verifying current-state correctness against Phase B prerequisites read THIS packet.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:173:4. **Heterogeneous swarm = Phase D, not B/C**. Per F-2026-04-25-02 + the `agent_models.rs` `PHASE_D_HETERO_GATE_ENV_VAR` invariant, Phases B and C MUST stay single-model so ablation axes are not confounded. A7's plumbing exists for future Phase D work; Phase B uses the existing `deepseek-v4-flash` thinking-off backbone unchanged.

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:227:**Why this doc exists** (A8e7 structural rewrite, 2026-04-26): rounds 1–6 of the Phase A → B audit produced a recurring documentary CHALLENGE pattern. Diagnosis: `A8_EXIT_PACKET` was conflating two opposite temporal modes (stable-snapshot artifact + append-only chronology) in one document, so every fix to "current state" generated new staleness in "round-N retrospective" sections. Splitting the two modes into two documents removes the category error.

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:5327:| `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` Rust→Python wrapper (A8e2 fix G1) | `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` | A8 round-2 audit (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1): the round-robin conformance battery in `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` was only manual; tests that don't run automatically are documentation, not gates. G1 wires the Python suite into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust integration test that shells to `python3` and asserts exit 0 + the unittest "OK" trailer. The V3L-27 mitigation (case C-027) is now a recurring conformance gate exercised on every commit that touches Rust + every CI pipeline that already exercises Rust tests. | ✅ |

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:9983:11:**Document split (A8e7)**: this packet is the **stable current-state snapshot** of Phase A at exit. The chronological history of audit rounds 1–N + their in-cycle fix bundles lives in the **companion** document `handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md` (append-only). Past audit transcripts are at `handover/audits/{CODEX,GEMINI}_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26{,_R2,_R3,_R4,_R5,_R6,...}.md`. Reviewers needing the full chronology read the history doc; reviewers verifying current-state correctness against Phase B prerequisites read THIS packet.

./handover/audits/EXT_GEMINI_PHASE_8_R1R8_2026-04-22.md:12:### R2 — VETO cleared?
./handover/audits/EXT_GEMINI_PHASE_8_R1R8_2026-04-22.md:37:All VETO and CHALLENGE items from the prior dual audit have been comprehensively and correctly addressed. The two critical VETOs on receipt forgeability (R1) and oneshot durability (R2) are fully resolved with robust fixes. All challenges are cleared with correct logic, extensive new tests, and improved documentation. The amendment is of high quality and can proceed to Phase 2 A/B testing.
./handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:145:PASS only if the batch result will be defensible to an external observer at Phase B → C audit packet review. The audit gate exists to PREVENT the user from spending $3-5 + 8h on a result that is later found to be invalid.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:82:- Codex R2: **CHALLENGE / high** — 3 findings (F2 not recurring; PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8 still says "strictest plausible bar" contradicting § 2; A8 packet + TRACE_MATRIX stale on counts + risk #5 + run_corr_id symbol row + "in CI" claim).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:83:- Gemini R2: **CHALLENGE / high** — 2 findings convergent with Codex (F2 needs CI integration; packet § 5 risk #5 stale).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:88:- **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1, hardened by A8e3 H6): `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` — Rust integration test that shells to `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` + asserts exit 0 + checks for the unittest "OK" trailer. Now exercised on every `cargo test --workspace` (PASS in A8e2/A8e3 verification). The Python suite is no longer "manual only" — it runs whenever the Rust tests run, which is every commit that touches Rust + every CI pipeline that already exercises Rust tests. **A8e3 H6 fail-closed**: missing `python3` is now a hard test failure (not a soft skip — Codex R3#3 caught the silent-pass risk). Explicit opt-out only via `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` and the bypass is logged loudly. Closes the round-2 "recurring conformance gate" finding.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:89:- **G2** (Codex R2#2): `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8` audit-requirements paragraph reworded to remove the residual "strictest plausible bar is conservative" phrase that contradicted § 2's wording correction. Re-hashed in Trust Root.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:90:- **G3** (Codex R2#3 + Gemini R2#2): A8 packet § 2 cumulative-test table extended with A8e + A8e2 columns; § 4 Trust-Root-clean assertion bumped 30→34; § 5 Risk #5 removed (closed by F4); § 6 Q7.a 261→265 + Q7.b 30→34. TRACE_MATRIX stale `run_corr_id` symbol row replaced; "in CI" softened to "via Rust wrapper post-A8e2".
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:184:- Substitutes `p_0 = 0.10` (PREREG § 5.5 ceiling) for the calibration-derived value at every Gate H consumer. The substitution is operationally permitted (the PREREG explicitly admits up to 0.10 as the ceiling) but is the **least-strict admissible value** since `j-RR ≤ p_0` makes a SMALLER `p_0` stricter — see PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 wording correction (round-1 audit Codex#1, A8e fix F6) for full statistical implications. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family. May be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`; acceptable because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first. Re-calibration conditions in § 3 list 5 items (N-experiments arc complete / swarm_N=1 mode landed / per-agent budget normalization landed / hetero-LLM exp complete / Phase D ArchitectAI runtime exists).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:243:4. **Heterogeneous swarm = Phase D, not B/C**. Per F-2026-04-25-02 + the `agent_models.rs` `PHASE_D_HETERO_GATE_ENV_VAR` invariant, Phases B and C MUST stay single-model so ablation axes are not confounded. A7's plumbing exists for future Phase D work; Phase B uses the existing `deepseek-v4-flash` thinking-off backbone unchanged.

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:357:5. **Phase D ArchitectAI runtime exists** — calibration is part of Gate H gating Phase E. Doing it before Phase D = calibrating against a counterfactual ArchitectAI that doesn't exist.

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5158:| `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` Rust→Python wrapper (A8e2 fix G1) | `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` | A8 round-2 audit (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1): the round-robin conformance battery in `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` was only manual; tests that don't run automatically are documentation, not gates. G1 wires the Python suite into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust integration test that shells to `python3` and asserts exit 0 + the unittest "OK" trailer. The Python suite is no longer "manual only" — it runs whenever the Rust tests run, which is every commit that touches Rust + every CI pipeline that already exercises Rust tests. **A8e3 H6 fail-closed**: missing `python3` is now a hard test failure (not a soft skip — Codex R3#3 caught the silent-pass risk). ./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5884:   144	- Substitutes `p_0 = 0.10` (PREREG § 5.5 ceiling) for the calibration-derived value at every Gate H consumer. The substitution is operationally permitted (the PREREG explicitly admits up to 0.10 as the ceiling) but is the **least-strict admissible value** since `j-RR ≤ p_0` makes a SMALLER `p_0` stricter — see PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 wording correction (round-1 audit Codex#1, A8e fix F6) for full statistical implications. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family. May be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`; acceptable because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first. Re-calibration conditions in § 3 list 5 items (N-experiments arc complete / swarm_N=1 mode landed / per-agent budget normalization landed / hetero-LLM exp complete / Phase D ArchitectAI runtime exists).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5943:   203	4. **Heterogeneous swarm = Phase D, not B/C**. Per F-2026-04-25-02 + the `agent_models.rs` `PHASE_D_HETERO_GATE_ENV_VAR` invariant, Phases B and C MUST stay single-model so ablation axes are not confounded. A7's plumbing exists for future Phase D work; Phase B uses the existing `deepseek-v4-flash` thinking-off backbone unchanged.

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:5430:- A8e9 (post-A8e8, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-8 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS): three fixes including one substantive PREREG logic miss. **N1 (substantive Codex R8#1)**: PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8 audit-requirements text still contained the stale claim "Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first" — round-7 M4 fixed § 2(b)'s identical false claim but missed the parallel text in § 8. Now reworded so § 2 + § 3 + § 8 all consistently express "substitution operationally permitted at any phase including Phase E; § 3 conditions are pre-requisites for calibration to run, not guarantees of completion before any phase". Re-hashed in Trust Root. **N2 (Codex R8#2)**: A8_AUDIT_HISTORY round-7 entry stamped with actual A8e8 SHA `857872e` (was `<aaedc9d-successor>` placeholder); cumulative table row 7 sealed (CHALLENGE/CHALLENGE; 6 findings; 1 substantive); row 8 added (CHALLENGE/PASS; 3 findings; 1 substantive); row 9 left as pending. **N3 (Codex R8#3)**: audit runner script header metadata refreshed — was emitting "264 PASS + 30-entry manifest" (round-1 era values) every time the runners executed; now emits current state (265 PASS / 35-entry manifest) and references the history doc for chronology. Trust Root manifest unchanged at 35. **Real-bug yield through 8 rounds: 9 substantive findings caught + closed** (5 routing/correctness in R1, 1 fail-closed gate in R3, 1 routing collision in R6, 1 PREREG § 2 logic in R7, 1 PREREG § 8 logic in R8).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:5437:- A8e2 (post-A8e, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-2 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent): three dual-audit fixes. **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1) `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` wraps `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` in a Rust integration test, making the V3L-27 round-robin conformance gate run on every `cargo test --workspace`. **G2** (Codex R2#2) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8` audit-requirements paragraph reworded — round-1 fix corrected § 2 but left § 8 still calling the substitution a "strictest plausible bar"; now consistent across the doc. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **G3** (Codex R2#3 + Gemini R2#2) `A8_EXIT_PACKET` updated for round-2 reality: § 2 cumulative-tests table extended with A8e/A8e2 columns, § 4 manifest-clean assertion bumped 30→34, § 5 round-1 risk #5 (no FC1-N12 in swarm verify path) removed (closed by F4), § 6 Q7.a/b numbers updated, round-2 outcome + round-3 fixes section appended; TRACE_MATRIX stale `run_corr_id` symbol row replaced; "in CI" softened to "via Rust wrapper post-A8e2". FC-trace: meta governance + V3L-27 mitigation gating (FC1-N7). Trust Root manifest 33 → 34 (`tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs`).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:5487:| `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` Rust→Python wrapper (A8e2 fix G1) | `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` | A8 round-2 audit (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1): the round-robin conformance battery in `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` was only manual; tests that don't run automatically are documentation, not gates. G1 wires the Python suite into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust integration test that shells to `python3` and asserts exit 0 + the unittest "OK" trailer. The V3L-27 mitigation (case C-027) is now a recurring conformance gate exercised on every commit that touches Rust + every CI pipeline that already exercises Rust tests. | ✅ |

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7480:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:184:- Substitutes `p_0 = 0.10` (PREREG § 5.5 ceiling) for the calibration-derived value at every Gate H consumer. The substitution is operationally permitted (the PREREG explicitly admits up to 0.10 as the ceiling) but is the **least-strict admissible value** since `j-RR ≤ p_0` makes a SMALLER `p_0` stricter — see PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 wording correction (round-1 audit Codex#1, A8e fix F6) for full statistical implications. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family. May be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`; acceptable because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first. Re-calibration conditions in § 3 list 5 items (N-experiments arc complete / swarm_N=1 mode landed / per-agent budget normalization landed / hetero-LLM exp complete / Phase D ArchitectAI runtime exists).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7481:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:189:- New `parse_swarm_condition_n` in `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs` discriminates `n<digits>` from `oneshot` / `hybrid_v1` / malformed. PREREG_AMENDMENT § 3 condition 2 cleared.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7482:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:212:- New module `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/fc_trace.rs`. Pure stdlib (zero new deps). 7-variant `FcId` enum (FC1-N7 / FC1-N11 / FC1-N12 / FC1-E18 / FC2-N20 / FC2-N22 / FC3-N31). `FC_TRACE=1` gate cached in `OnceLock`; `FC_TRACE_FILE=<path>` redirects emit to file.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7483:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:213:- 9 wired anchor sites total (round-1 had 6 — A8e fix F4 added 3 swarm verify sites): synthetic short-circuit / mr tick / OMEGA full-proof / OMEGA per-tactic / natural MaxTxExhausted (with budget_regime payload from A5) / oneshot FC1-N12 verify bracket / **swarm `verify_omega_detailed` × 2 paths (alone + tape+payload retry)** / **swarm `verify_partial`**.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7484:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:220:- `src/drivers/llm_proxy.py` ported from v3 with one load-bearing v4 change: per-provider multi-key round-robin. PROVIDERS map now holds a list of env names per provider; `get_client_round_robin` distributes via `_rr_counters` mod `len(clients)`. `/stats` exposes `per_key_requests` for observability. New `siliconflow:<model>` provider-prefix syntax.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7485:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:245:(Round-1 risk #5 "No FC1-N12 emit in run_swarm verify path" was CLOSED by A8e fix F4 — both auditors flagged the staleness in round-2 (Codex R2#3, Gemini R2#2). ./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7486:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:250:- (Q1.a) Each of A1–A7 was committed as one atomic commit with `FC-trace: <FC?-N?>` in the commit message, per memory `feedback_fc_first_problem_handling`. Verify by re-reading each commit message. Are any commits missing FC-trace, or claiming an FC node that doesn't match the actual code change?
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7487:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:334:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7488:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:338:- The substitution may be LESS protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`. When calibration runs (per § 3 conditions), the empirical estimate likely tightens the bar; until then we operate at the ceiling.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7489:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:392:"handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md" = "<sha256>"
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7490:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:402:- Does substitution of `p_0 = 0.10` invalidate any Gate H statistical claim? (Should not — `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family, so no Type-I inflation. Per § 2 wording correction: 0.10 is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling, NOT a tighter-than-original substitute — the substitution may be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`, accepted because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first.)

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7631:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:5105:- A6 (post-A5): per-line FC tagging via structured JSON events — new `fc_trace` module (pure stdlib; zero new deps); `FcId` enum (FC1-N7 / FC1-N11 / FC1-N12 / FC1-E18 / FC2-N20 / FC2-N22 / FC3-N31); `fc_event!`-style `emit_event` API; `FC_TRACE=1` gate (cached in `OnceLock`); `FC_TRACE_FILE=<path>` redirects emit to file (default sink stderr). Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`): FC2-N22 synthetic short-circuit (swarm), FC2-N20 mr tick (swarm), FC2-N22 OMEGA full-proof (swarm), FC2-N22 OMEGA per-tactic (swarm), FC2-N22 natural MaxTxExhausted (swarm, with `budget_regime` payload), FC1-N12 verify bracket (oneshot). A8e fix F4 added 3 more in `run_swarm` (verify_omega_detailed × 2 + verify_partial), bringing the post-A8e total to 9 production emit sites. End-to-end smoke test exercises FC_TRACE=1 in a child process (subprocess required because `OnceLock` caches the gate-read; resolves item 7 of TRACE_MATRIX § 5 "Per-line FC tagging via tracing crate"). FC-trace: meta-witness for the 5-step compile loop (Proposal → Lean ground truth → Logging → Capability compilation → ↑H-VPPUT). Trust Root manifest 26 → 27.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7632:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:5106:- A7 (post-A6): heterogeneous-LLM provider plumbing — `src/drivers/llm_proxy.py` ported from v3 with one load-bearing v4 change (per-provider multi-key round-robin: 3 SiliconFlow keys split concurrent traffic across separate rate-limit pools, mitigating V3L-27 single-key N=30 collapse). `scripts/smoke_siliconflow.sh` + `scripts/_smoke_siliconflow.py` probe each of the 3 keys (Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct, max_tokens=8) — A7 verified all 3 keys responding 2026-04-26 (1.5–3s latency, 33+1 tokens; round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls). New `siliconflow:<model>` provider-prefix syntax in `detect_provider()` for unambiguous routing in `AGENT_MODELS` payloads (Phase D heterogeneous swarms). Memory `reference_siliconflow.md` records SiliconFlow as the heterogeneous-LLM lane (NOT a fallback target). FC-trace: FC1-N7 (δ/AI provider expansion — heterogeneous δ instances across SF catalog enable Phase D meta-loop). Trust Root manifest 28 → 31 (proxy + 2 smoke scripts).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7633:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:5107:- A8e4 (post-A8e3, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-4 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — split verdict, conservative merge wins per memory `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`): three narrow documentary defects Codex found in the packet itself. **I1** packet title + date: "round 2" → "running through rounds 1–N" (cumulative, with reader pointer to most-recent round section). **I2** per-atom Trust Root deltas in § 3: A5 25→26 → 26→27, A6 26→27 → 27→28, A7 27→30 → 28→31 (all corrected to match the chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header — round-1 packet undercounted because A3's `agent_models.rs` had already raised the count to 26 before A5 landed); TRACE_MATRIX § 1 line 11 anchor-site location: "Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`" → "Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`)" — same in § 5 item 7 closure note. **I3** `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` file header docstring: "if not, it skips with a clear diagnostic" → "if not, it FAILS CLOSED with a clear diagnostic" (A8e3 H6 fixed the runtime but left the docstring stale; Codex R4#3 caught the contradiction). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hash of the wrapper). No source-code defects, no architectural changes; this round is purely documentary alignment.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7634:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:5108:- A8e3 (post-A8e2, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-3 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on packet staleness; no VETO): six narrow cleanup fixes. **H1** A8 packet § 3 A1 description: "Mathematically conservative" → "least-strict admissible value" with explicit Type-I implications. **H2** packet § 3 A6 description: 6 → 9 anchor sites (added F4 swarm sites explicitly). **H3** packet § 6 Q2.a / Q4.a / Q4.d marked CLOSED with closure rationale. **H4** `genesis_payload.toml` TR header phrasing about A1: "conservative ceiling" → "max-tolerated ceiling". **H5** § 5 item 7 here in TRACE_MATRIX: "(commit pending) + 6 wired" → "CLOSED + 9 wired". **H6** G1 wrapper test fail-closed on missing `python3` (was soft skip; Codex R3 caught the silent-pass risk); explicit opt-out `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` for deliberate downgrades (logged loudly). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hashes; no new files). Non-blocking observation deferred to Phase B+: `make_pput` signature is now 21 positional args (Gemini R3 Finding 2) — refactor to a builder pattern is recommended but not gating.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7635:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:5109:- A8e2 (post-A8e, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-2 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent): three dual-audit fixes. **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1) `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` wraps `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` in a Rust integration test, making the V3L-27 round-robin conformance gate run on every `cargo test --workspace`. **G2** (Codex R2#2) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8` audit-requirements paragraph reworded — round-1 fix corrected § 2 but left § 8 still calling the substitution a "strictest plausible bar"; now consistent across the doc. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **G3** (Codex R2#3 + Gemini R2#2) `A8_EXIT_PACKET` updated for round-2 reality: § 2 cumulative-tests table extended with A8e/A8e2 columns, § 4 manifest-clean assertion bumped 30→34, § 5 round-1 risk #5 (no FC1-N12 in swarm verify path) removed (closed by F4), § 6 Q7.a/b numbers updated, round-2 outcome + round-3 fixes section appended; TRACE_MATRIX stale `run_corr_id` symbol row replaced; "in CI" softened to "via Rust wrapper post-A8e2". FC-trace: meta governance + V3L-27 mitigation gating (FC1-N7). Trust Root manifest 33 → 34 (`tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs`).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7636:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:5110:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7658:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:5604:   144	- Substitutes `p_0 = 0.10` (PREREG § 5.5 ceiling) for the calibration-derived value at every Gate H consumer. The substitution is operationally permitted (the PREREG explicitly admits up to 0.10 as the ceiling) but is the **least-strict admissible value** since `j-RR ≤ p_0` makes a SMALLER `p_0` stricter — see PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 wording correction (round-1 audit Codex#1, A8e fix F6) for full statistical implications. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family. May be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`; acceptable because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first. Re-calibration conditions in § 3 list 5 items (N-experiments arc complete / swarm_N=1 mode landed / per-agent budget normalization landed / hetero-LLM exp complete / Phase D ArchitectAI runtime exists).

PREREG_AMENDMENT § 3 condition 2 cleared.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7660:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:5632:   172	- New module `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/fc_trace.rs`. Pure stdlib (zero new deps). 7-variant `FcId` enum (FC1-N7 / FC1-N11 / FC1-N12 / FC1-E18 / FC2-N20 / FC2-N22 / FC3-N31). `FC_TRACE=1` gate cached in `OnceLock`; `FC_TRACE_FILE=<path>` redirects emit to file.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7661:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:5633:   173	- 9 wired anchor sites total (round-1 had 6 — A8e fix F4 added 3 swarm verify sites): synthetic short-circuit / mr tick / OMEGA full-proof / OMEGA per-tactic / natural MaxTxExhausted (with budget_regime payload from A5) / oneshot FC1-N12 verify bracket / **swarm `verify_omega_detailed` × 2 paths (alone + tape+payload retry)** / **swarm `verify_partial`**.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7662:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:5640:   180	- `src/drivers/llm_proxy.py` ported from v3 with one load-bearing v4 change: per-provider multi-key round-robin. PROVIDERS map now holds a list of env names per provider; `get_client_round_robin` distributes via `_rr_counters` mod `len(clients)`. `/stats` exposes `per_key_requests` for observability. New `siliconflow:<model>` provider-prefix syntax.

Removed from this list.)
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7664:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:5670:   210	- (Q1.a) Each of A1–A7 was committed as one atomic commit with `FC-trace: <FC?-N?>` in the commit message, per memory `feedback_fc_first_problem_handling`. Verify by re-reading each commit message. Are any commits missing FC-trace, or claiming an FC node that doesn't match the actual code change?
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7665:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:5751:    27	**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7666:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:5755:    31	- The substitution may be LESS protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`. When calibration runs (per § 3 conditions), the empirical estimate likely tightens the bar; until then we operate at the ceiling.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7667:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:5809:    85	"handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md" = "<sha256>"

(Should not — `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family, so no Type-I inflation. Per § 2 wording correction: 0.10 is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling, NOT a tighter-than-original substitute — the substitution may be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`, accepted because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first.)

Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hash of the wrapper). No source-code defects, no architectural changes; this round is purely documentary alignment.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7711:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:6750:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:14:- A8e3 (post-A8e2, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-3 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on packet staleness; no VETO): six narrow cleanup fixes. **H1** A8 packet § 3 A1 description: "Mathematically conservative" → "least-strict admissible value" with explicit Type-I implications. **H2** packet § 3 A6 description: 6 → 9 anchor sites (added F4 swarm sites explicitly). **H3** packet § 6 Q2.a / Q4.a / Q4.d marked CLOSED with closure rationale. **H4** `genesis_payload.toml` TR header phrasing about A1: "conservative ceiling" → "max-tolerated ceiling". **H5** § 5 item 7 here in TRACE_MATRIX: "(commit pending) + 6 wired" → "CLOSED + 9 wired". **H6** G1 wrapper test fail-closed on missing `python3` (was soft skip; Codex R3 caught the silent-pass risk); explicit opt-out `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` for deliberate downgrades (logged loudly). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hashes; no new files). Non-blocking observation deferred to Phase B+: `make_pput` signature is now 21 positional args (Gemini R3 Finding 2) — refactor to a builder pattern is recommended but not gating.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7712:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:6751:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:15:- A8e2 (post-A8e, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-2 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent): three dual-audit fixes. **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1) `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` wraps `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` in a Rust integration test, making the V3L-27 round-robin conformance gate run on every `cargo test --workspace`. **G2** (Codex R2#2) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8` audit-requirements paragraph reworded — round-1 fix corrected § 2 but left § 8 still calling the substitution a "strictest plausible bar"; now consistent across the doc. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **G3** (Codex R2#3 + Gemini R2#2) `A8_EXIT_PACKET` updated for round-2 reality: § 2 cumulative-tests table extended with A8e/A8e2 columns, § 4 manifest-clean assertion bumped 30→34, § 5 round-1 risk #5 (no FC1-N12 in swarm verify path) removed (closed by F4), § 6 Q7.a/b numbers updated, round-2 outcome + round-3 fixes section appended; TRACE_MATRIX stale `run_corr_id` symbol row replaced; "in CI" softened to "via Rust wrapper post-A8e2". FC-trace: meta governance + V3L-27 mitigation gating (FC1-N7). Trust Root manifest 33 → 34 (`tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs`).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7713:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:6752:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:16:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7714:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:6754:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:27:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7715:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:6756:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:95:- Does substitution of `p_0 = 0.10` invalidate any Gate H statistical claim? (Should not — `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family, so no Type-I inflation. Per § 2 wording correction: 0.10 is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling, NOT a tighter-than-original substitute — the substitution may be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`, accepted because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first.)
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7716:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:6760:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:42:- Codex R2: **CHALLENGE / high** — 3 findings (F2 not recurring; PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8 still says "strictest plausible bar" contradicting § 2; A8 packet + TRACE_MATRIX stale on counts + risk #5 + run_corr_id symbol row + "in CI" claim).

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7718:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:6762:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:55:- (RQ7) Verify G2: re-grep `PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md` for any remaining "strictest" / "conservative" claim; the only acceptable use is the round-1 wording-correction note in § 2.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7719:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:6766:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:80:- (RQ10) Verify H1: re-grep `A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md` for any remaining "conservative" / "strictest" claim about `p_0`. ./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7720:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:6767:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:83:- (RQ13) Verify packet self-consistency: any other "conservative" claims about the substitution? Any other anchor-count mismatches? Any other contradictions between round-1 questions and round-2/3 closures?
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7721:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:6768:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:144:- Substitutes `p_0 = 0.10` (PREREG § 5.5 ceiling) for the calibration-derived value at every Gate H consumer. The substitution is operationally permitted (the PREREG explicitly admits up to 0.10 as the ceiling) but is the **least-strict admissible value** since `j-RR ≤ p_0` makes a SMALLER `p_0` stricter — see PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 wording correction (round-1 audit Codex#1, A8e fix F6) for full statistical implications. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family. May be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`; acceptable because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first. Re-calibration conditions in § 3 list 5 items (N-experiments arc complete / swarm_N=1 mode landed / per-agent budget normalization landed / hetero-LLM exp complete / Phase D ArchitectAI runtime exists).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7722:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:6830:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R2.md

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7725:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:6851:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:11:- A6 (post-A5): per-line FC tagging via structured JSON events — new `fc_trace` module (pure stdlib; zero new deps); `FcId` enum (FC1-N7 / FC1-N11 / FC1-N12 / FC1-E18 / FC2-N20 / FC2-N22 / FC3-N31); `fc_event!`-style `emit_event` API; `FC_TRACE=1` gate (cached in `OnceLock`); `FC_TRACE_FILE=<path>` redirects emit to file (default sink stderr). Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`): FC2-N22 synthetic short-circuit (swarm), FC2-N20 mr tick (swarm), FC2-N22 OMEGA full-proof (swarm), FC2-N22 OMEGA per-tactic (swarm), FC2-N22 natural MaxTxExhausted (swarm, with `budget_regime` payload), FC1-N12 verify bracket (oneshot). A8e fix F4 added 3 more in `run_swarm` (verify_omega_detailed × 2 + verify_partial), bringing the post-A8e total to 9 production emit sites. End-to-end smoke test exercises FC_TRACE=1 in a child process (subprocess required because `OnceLock` caches the gate-read; resolves item 7 of TRACE_MATRIX § 5 "Per-line FC tagging via tracing crate"). The Python suite is no longer "manual only" — it runs whenever the Rust tests run, which is every commit that touches Rust + every CI pipeline that already exercises Rust tests. **A8e3 H6 fail-closed**: missing `python3` is now a hard test failure (not a soft skip — Codex R3#3 caught the silent-pass risk). Explicit opt-out only via `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` and the bypass is logged loudly. Closes the round-2 "recurring conformance gate" finding.

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7763:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:8124:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:8:- A0d (this doc): Trust Root manifest 20 → 24 (this commit); v2 documents the harness as constitutional artifact
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7764:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:8126:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:11:- A6 (post-A5): per-line FC tagging via structured JSON events — new `fc_trace` module (pure stdlib; zero new deps); `FcId` enum (FC1-N7 / FC1-N11 / FC1-N12 / FC1-E18 / FC2-N20 / FC2-N22 / FC3-N31); `fc_event!`-style `emit_event` API; `FC_TRACE=1` gate (cached in `OnceLock`); `FC_TRACE_FILE=<path>` redirects emit to file (default sink stderr). Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`): FC2-N22 synthetic short-circuit (swarm), FC2-N20 mr tick (swarm), FC2-N22 OMEGA full-proof (swarm), FC2-N22 OMEGA per-tactic (swarm), FC2-N22 natural MaxTxExhausted (swarm, with `budget_regime` payload), FC1-N12 verify bracket (oneshot). A8e fix F4 added 3 more in `run_swarm` (verify_omega_detailed × 2 + verify_partial), bringing the post-A8e total to 9 production emit sites. End-to-end smoke test exercises FC_TRACE=1 in a child process (subprocess required because `OnceLock` caches the gate-read; resolves item 7 of TRACE_MATRIX § 5 "Per-line FC tagging via tracing crate"). FC-trace: meta-witness for the 5-step compile loop (Proposal → Lean ground truth → Logging → Capability compilation → ↑H-VPPUT). Trust Root manifest 26 → 27.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7765:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:8127:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:12:- A7 (post-A6): heterogeneous-LLM provider plumbing — `src/drivers/llm_proxy.py` ported from v3 with one load-bearing v4 change (per-provider multi-key round-robin: 3 SiliconFlow keys split concurrent traffic across separate rate-limit pools, mitigating V3L-27 single-key N=30 collapse). `scripts/smoke_siliconflow.sh` + `scripts/_smoke_siliconflow.py` probe each of the 3 keys (Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct, max_tokens=8) — A7 verified all 3 keys responding 2026-04-26 (1.5–3s latency, 33+1 tokens; round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls). New `siliconflow:<model>` provider-prefix syntax in `detect_provider()` for unambiguous routing in `AGENT_MODELS` payloads (Phase D heterogeneous swarms). Memory `reference_siliconflow.md` records SiliconFlow as the heterogeneous-LLM lane (NOT a fallback target). FC-trace: FC1-N7 (δ/AI provider expansion — heterogeneous δ instances across SF catalog enable Phase D meta-loop). Trust Root manifest 28 → 31 (proxy + 2 smoke scripts).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7766:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:8128:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:15:- A8e2 (post-A8e, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-2 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent): three dual-audit fixes. **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1) `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` wraps `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` in a Rust integration test, making the V3L-27 round-robin conformance gate run on every `cargo test --workspace`. **G2** (Codex R2#2) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8` audit-requirements paragraph reworded — round-1 fix corrected § 2 but left § 8 still calling the substitution a "strictest plausible bar"; now consistent across the doc. ./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7767:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:8129:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:16:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7768:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:8142:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:27:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7769:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:8143:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:13:- A8e4 (post-A8e3, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-4 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — split verdict, conservative merge wins per memory `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`): three narrow documentary defects Codex found in the packet itself. **I1** packet title + date: "round 2" → "running through rounds 1–N" (cumulative, with reader pointer to most-recent round section). **I2** per-atom Trust Root deltas in § 3: A5 25→26 → 26→27, A6 26→27 → 27→28, A7 27→30 → 28→31 (all corrected to match the chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header — round-1 packet undercounted because A3's `agent_models.rs` had already raised the count to 26 before A5 landed); TRACE_MATRIX § 1 line 11 anchor-site location: "Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`" → "Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`)" — same in § 5 item 7 closure note. **I3** `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` file header docstring: "if not, it skips with a clear diagnostic" → "if not, it FAILS CLOSED with a clear diagnostic" (A8e3 H6 fixed the runtime but left the docstring stale; Codex R4#3 caught the contradiction). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hash of the wrapper). No source-code defects, no architectural changes; this round is purely documentary alignment.

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7784:handover/audits/GEMINI_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R2.md:5:**Test baseline**: 264 PASS + 29 ignored + 0 failed (Rust); 15/15 PASS (Python proxy tests)
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7785:handover/audits/GEMINI_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R2.md:18:I have reviewed the A8 packet, the six fixes (F1–F6), and the associated source code. The fixes substantively address the letter of the five findings from Codex and the VETO-level finding from Gemini in the round-1 audit. The unification of `run_id` (F1), the correction of the routing matrix (F3), and the improved wording of the PREREG amendment (F6) are particularly well-executed.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7786:handover/audits/GEMINI_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R2.md:24:The round-1 VETO was predicated on the absence of an automated test to pin the `llm_proxy.py` round-robin invariant, a critical mitigation for the V3L-27 collapse. Fix F2 (`scripts/test_llm_proxy.py`) provides the necessary test artifact, and its inclusion in the Trust Root (per `genesis_payload.toml`) prevents its silent modification.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7787:handover/audits/GEMINI_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R2.md:54:The fixes are substantial and address the core technical issues from the round-1 audit. The remaining defects are procedural and documentary, not architectural. They can and must be corrected in-cycle before Phase B is authorized. ./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8063:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:436:| 7 | pending | pending | pending | — | — | ~5 |
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8064:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:475:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8065:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:479:- The substitution may be LESS protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`. If/when calibration runs (per § 3 conditions, which are PRE-REQUISITES — they do not by themselves *guarantee* calibration completes before any specific phase), the empirical estimate may tighten the bar; until then Gate H operates at the ceiling.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8066:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:480:- Acceptable trade-off because (a) Phase B and C don't gate on `j-RR` (Gate H is a Phase E artifact gate), and (b) the substitution is **operationally permitted at any phase including Phase E** — see § 8 for the operative rule. If § 3 conditions complete before Phase E, calibration UPGRADES the bar; if not, Phase E proceeds with the ceiling substitution. There is no path under this amendment where Phase E is blocked by absence of calibration — that was an earlier draft's misclaim, removed by A8e8 fix M4 after Codex round-7 audit.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8067:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:533:"handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md" = "<sha256>"
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8068:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:543:- Does substitution of `p_0 = 0.10` invalidate any Gate H statistical claim? (Should not — `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family, so no Type-I inflation. Per § 2 wording correction: 0.10 is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling, NOT a tighter-than-original substitute — the substitution may be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`, accepted because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first.)

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8213:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5401:- A5 (post-A4): per-agent budget normalization — new `budget_regime` module (`BUDGET_REGIME` + `MAX_TRANSACTIONS` env vars; 4-variant enum; pure parser + scaler + env-coupled resolver); `budget_regime` + `budget_max_transactions` added as non-Optional v2 fields on `RunAggregate` and the legacy `PputResult`; loop bound at `run_swarm` switched from hardcoded `let max_transactions = 200` to `resolve_budget(n_agents)` — default (env unset) preserves Phase B baseline (`total_proposal × 200`) bit-for-bit. Trust Root manifest 26 → 27 (corrected A8e5 J4 — the A1 PREREG amendment had taken the count to 25, A3's `agent_models.rs` to 26 before A5 landed; round-1 packet undercounted by 1).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8214:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5402:- A6 (post-A5): per-line FC tagging via structured JSON events — new `fc_trace` module (pure stdlib; zero new deps); `FcId` enum (FC1-N7 / FC1-N11 / FC1-N12 / FC1-E18 / FC2-N20 / FC2-N22 / FC3-N31); `fc_event!`-style `emit_event` API; `FC_TRACE=1` gate (cached in `OnceLock`); `FC_TRACE_FILE=<path>` redirects emit to file (default sink stderr). 9 anchor sites wired (post-A8e total — 8 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`): the original A6 commit shipped 6 sites (FC2-N22 synthetic short-circuit, FC2-N20 mr tick, FC2-N22 OMEGA full-proof, FC2-N22 OMEGA per-tactic, FC2-N22 natural MaxTxExhausted with `budget_regime` payload, FC1-N12 verify bracket in oneshot). A8e fix F4 added 3 more in `run_swarm` (FC1-N12 verify_omega_detailed × 2 paths + FC1-N12 verify_partial), bringing the post-A8e total to 9 production emit sites. Counted by grepping `fc_trace::emit_event(` in `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs` (Gemini R6 finding: round-5 phrasing "Six anchor sites" was stale relative to its own following sentence — corrected here in A8e6 K4). End-to-end smoke test exercises FC_TRACE=1 in a child process (subprocess required because `OnceLock` caches the gate-read; resolves item 7 of TRACE_MATRIX § 5 "Per-line FC tagging via tracing crate"). FC-trace: meta-witness for the 5-step compile loop (Proposal → Lean ground truth → Logging → Capability compilation → ↑H-VPPUT). Trust Root manifest 27 → 28 (corrected A8e5 J4; matches the canonical chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8215:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5403:- A7 (post-A6): heterogeneous-LLM provider plumbing — `src/drivers/llm_proxy.py` ported from v3 with one load-bearing v4 change (per-provider multi-key round-robin: 3 SiliconFlow keys split concurrent traffic across separate rate-limit pools, mitigating V3L-27 single-key N=30 collapse). `scripts/smoke_siliconflow.sh` + `scripts/_smoke_siliconflow.py` probe each of the 3 keys (Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct, max_tokens=8) — A7 verified all 3 keys responding 2026-04-26 (1.5–3s latency, 33+1 tokens; round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls). New `siliconflow:<model>` provider-prefix syntax in `detect_provider()` for unambiguous routing in `AGENT_MODELS` payloads (Phase D heterogeneous swarms). Memory `reference_siliconflow.md` records SiliconFlow as the heterogeneous-LLM lane (NOT a fallback target). FC-trace: FC1-N7 (δ/AI provider expansion — heterogeneous δ instances across SF catalog enable Phase D meta-loop). Trust Root manifest 28 → 31 (3 entries: `llm_proxy.py` + 2 smoke scripts).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8216:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5404:- A8e8 (post-A8e7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-7 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on rewrite-implementation gaps): five fixes including one substantive PREREG logic correction. **M1** packet § 2 + § 4 Trust Root count bumped 34 → 35 (post-A8e7 added `A8_AUDIT_HISTORY` to manifest). **M2** this history doc's A8e7 entry stamped with actual SHA `aaedc9d` + round-7 verdicts + A8e8 fix list (round-7 sealed). **M3** packet rewritten to strip ALL historical lineage text — no "(post-A8e F4)", "(added by A8eN)", "(chain position N via A8e)", "(+ A8eN fix XN)". Packet describes WHAT IS, no HOW; lineage lives only in this history doc. **M4 (substantive Codex R7#4)** PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2(b) corrected: removed false claim that "§ 3 conditions ensure calibration runs *before* Phase E"; § 3 conditions are PRE-REQUISITES for calibration TO RUN at all, not guarantees of completion before any specific phase. § 8 reads as the operative rule: substitution permitted at any phase including Phase E; calibration UPGRADES the bar IF and WHEN § 3 conditions complete. Single semantics, internally consistent across § 2 / § 3 / § 8. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **M5** audit runner scripts now append `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` so Q1.c (verify wrapper fail-closed) is verifiable from the packet bundle alone. Trust Root manifest unchanged at 35 (only re-hashes; no new files).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8217:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5406:- A8e6 (post-A8e5, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-6 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both narrow): one real correctness fix + 4 doc fixes. **K1** packet `<pending>` placeholder cleanup (J2 + RQ14 wording) — same precedent as A8e5 J2 but applied to round-6 self-references. **K2 (real bug, Codex R6#2)** `src/drivers/llm_proxy.py` `detect_provider`: HuggingFace-style `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B` was misrouting to `api.deepseek.com` because `"deepseek" in m` won before the slash check; pre-K2 logic would 404 on every Distill variant since the official DeepSeek API doesn't serve them. K2 swaps the order — slash-form is now the FIRST routing heuristic (after explicit prefix), and bare-deepseek substring only fires when there's no slash. New `test_deepseek_slash_form_routes_to_siliconflow` in `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` pins this (covers `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B` + `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V2.5` AS WELL AS the bare-form sanity case `deepseek-chat` → deepseek). **K3** TRACE_MATRIX A8e3 row stale "21 positional args" → 24 (matches packet update from A8e5 J5; Codex R6#3 caught the inconsistency between TRACE_MATRIX and the packet). **K4** TRACE_MATRIX A6 row top-line "Six anchor sites" → "9 anchor sites" with A6-original-6 + F4-added-3 breakdown (Gemini R6#1 caught the contradiction between the line's first sentence and its own follow-up). **K5** packet § 3 A5/A6 fix attribution: "A8e3 fix H4 corrected" → "A8e4 fix I2 corrected" (Gemini R6#2 caught the cross-reference error — H4 corrected the genesis_payload.toml TR-header comment, I2 corrected the packet § 3 numbers). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (re-hashes of `llm_proxy.py` + `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py`; no new files).

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8222:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5411:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8240:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5735:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:120:- B7-extra round-1 audit-fix → 20
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8241:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5741:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:27:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8242:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5742:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:32:- Acceptable trade-off because (a) Phase B and C don't gate on `j-RR` (Gate H is a Phase E artifact gate), and (b) the substitution is **operationally permitted at any phase including Phase E** — see § 8 for the operative rule. If § 3 conditions complete before Phase E, calibration UPGRADES the bar; if not, Phase E proceeds with the ceiling substitution. There is no path under this amendment where Phase E is blocked by absence of calibration — that was an earlier draft's misclaim, removed by A8e8 fix M4 after Codex round-7 audit.

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8257:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5814:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:135:(All round-1..round-7 questions and their closures are recorded in `A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md`. The questions below are for the current-round reviewer pass.)
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8258:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5827:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:27:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8259:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5829:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:31:- The substitution may be LESS protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`. If/when calibration runs (per § 3 conditions, which are PRE-REQUISITES — they do not by themselves *guarantee* calibration completes before any specific phase), the empirical estimate may tighten the bar; until then Gate H operates at the ceiling.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8260:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5830:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:32:- Acceptable trade-off because (a) Phase B and C don't gate on `j-RR` (Gate H is a Phase E artifact gate), and (b) the substitution is **operationally permitted at any phase including Phase E** — see § 8 for the operative rule. If § 3 conditions complete before Phase E, calibration UPGRADES the bar; if not, Phase E proceeds with the ceiling substitution. There is no path under this amendment where Phase E is blocked by absence of calibration — that was an earlier draft's misclaim, removed by A8e8 fix M4 after Codex round-7 audit.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8261:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5835:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:95:- Does substitution of `p_0 = 0.10` invalidate any Gate H statistical claim? (Should not — `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family, so no Type-I inflation. Per § 2 wording correction: 0.10 is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling, NOT a tighter-than-original substitute — the substitution may be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`, accepted because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first.)
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8262:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5845:handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:57:2. (Codex R2#2) PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 was corrected (F6) but § 8 audit-requirements paragraph still says "strictest plausible bar is conservative" — direct contradiction with § 2.

But § 8 still says the substitution is accepted because “§ 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first”: [PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:95](</home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:95>). ./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8501:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:107:- **Statistical framing** (post-A8e F6 + A8e2 G2 wording corrections): `0.10` is the **least-strict admissible value** the PREREG ceiling allows; `j-RR ≤ p_0` makes a SMALLER `p_0` stricter, so this substitution is operationally permitted but NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee. ./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8527:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:399:**Round 7 dual audit pending after this commit lands.**
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8528:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:413:| 7 | pending | pending | pending | — | — | ~5 |
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8529:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:452:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8530:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:456:- The substitution may be LESS protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`. When calibration runs (per § 3 conditions), the empirical estimate likely tightens the bar; until then we operate at the ceiling.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8531:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:510:"handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md" = "<sha256>"
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8532:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:520:- Does substitution of `p_0 = 0.10` invalidate any Gate H statistical claim? (Should not — `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family, so no Type-I inflation. Per § 2 wording correction: 0.10 is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling, NOT a tighter-than-original substitute — the substitution may be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`, accepted because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first.)

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8674:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:5269:- A5 (post-A4): per-agent budget normalization — new `budget_regime` module (`BUDGET_REGIME` + `MAX_TRANSACTIONS` env vars; 4-variant enum; pure parser + scaler + env-coupled resolver); `budget_regime` + `budget_max_transactions` added as non-Optional v2 fields on `RunAggregate` and the legacy `PputResult`; loop bound at `run_swarm` switched from hardcoded `let max_transactions = 200` to `resolve_budget(n_agents)` — default (env unset) preserves Phase B baseline (`total_proposal × 200`) bit-for-bit. ./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8675:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:5270:- A6 (post-A5): per-line FC tagging via structured JSON events — new `fc_trace` module (pure stdlib; zero new deps); `FcId` enum (FC1-N7 / FC1-N11 / FC1-N12 / FC1-E18 / FC2-N20 / FC2-N22 / FC3-N31); `fc_event!`-style `emit_event` API; `FC_TRACE=1` gate (cached in `OnceLock`); `FC_TRACE_FILE=<path>` redirects emit to file (default sink stderr). 9 anchor sites wired (post-A8e total — 8 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`): the original A6 commit shipped 6 sites (FC2-N22 synthetic short-circuit, FC2-N20 mr tick, FC2-N22 OMEGA full-proof, FC2-N22 OMEGA per-tactic, FC2-N22 natural MaxTxExhausted with `budget_regime` payload, FC1-N12 verify bracket in oneshot). A8e fix F4 added 3 more in `run_swarm` (FC1-N12 verify_omega_detailed × 2 paths + FC1-N12 verify_partial), bringing the post-A8e total to 9 production emit sites. Counted by grepping `fc_trace::emit_event(` in `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs` (Gemini R6 finding: round-5 phrasing "Six anchor sites" was stale relative to its own following sentence — corrected here in A8e6 K4). End-to-end smoke test exercises FC_TRACE=1 in a child process (subprocess required because `OnceLock` caches the gate-read; resolves item 7 of TRACE_MATRIX § 5 "Per-line FC tagging via tracing crate"). FC-trace: meta-witness for the 5-step compile loop (Proposal → Lean ground truth → Logging → Capability compilation → ↑H-VPPUT). Trust Root manifest 27 → 28 (corrected A8e5 J4; matches the canonical chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8676:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:5271:- A7 (post-A6): heterogeneous-LLM provider plumbing — `src/drivers/llm_proxy.py` ported from v3 with one load-bearing v4 change (per-provider multi-key round-robin: 3 SiliconFlow keys split concurrent traffic across separate rate-limit pools, mitigating V3L-27 single-key N=30 collapse). `scripts/smoke_siliconflow.sh` + `scripts/_smoke_siliconflow.py` probe each of the 3 keys (Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct, max_tokens=8) — A7 verified all 3 keys responding 2026-04-26 (1.5–3s latency, 33+1 tokens; round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls). New `siliconflow:<model>` provider-prefix syntax in `detect_provider()` for unambiguous routing in `AGENT_MODELS` payloads (Phase D heterogeneous swarms). Memory `reference_siliconflow.md` records SiliconFlow as the heterogeneous-LLM lane (NOT a fallback target). FC-trace: FC1-N7 (δ/AI provider expansion — heterogeneous δ instances across SF catalog enable Phase D meta-loop). Trust Root manifest 28 → 31 (3 entries: `llm_proxy.py` + 2 smoke scripts).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8677:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:5273:- A8e6 (post-A8e5, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-6 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both narrow): one real correctness fix + 4 doc fixes. **K1** packet `<pending>` placeholder cleanup (J2 + RQ14 wording) — same precedent as A8e5 J2 but applied to round-6 self-references. **K2 (real bug, Codex R6#2)** `src/drivers/llm_proxy.py` `detect_provider`: HuggingFace-style `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B` was misrouting to `api.deepseek.com` because `"deepseek" in m` won before the slash check; pre-K2 logic would 404 on every Distill variant since the official DeepSeek API doesn't serve them. K2 swaps the order — slash-form is now the FIRST routing heuristic (after explicit prefix), and bare-deepseek substring only fires when there's no slash. New `test_deepseek_slash_form_routes_to_siliconflow` in `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` pins this (covers `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B` + `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V2.5` AS WELL AS the bare-form sanity case `deepseek-chat` → deepseek). **K3** TRACE_MATRIX A8e3 row stale "21 positional args" → 24 (matches packet update from A8e5 J5; Codex R6#3 caught the inconsistency between TRACE_MATRIX and the packet). **K4** TRACE_MATRIX A6 row top-line "Six anchor sites" → "9 anchor sites" with A6-original-6 + F4-added-3 breakdown (Gemini R6#1 caught the contradiction between the line's first sentence and its own follow-up). **K5** packet § 3 A5/A6 fix attribution: "A8e3 fix H4 corrected" → "A8e4 fix I2 corrected" (Gemini R6#2 caught the cross-reference error — H4 corrected the genesis_payload.toml TR-header comment, I2 corrected the packet § 3 numbers). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (re-hashes of `llm_proxy.py` + `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py`; no new files).
