./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8680:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:5276:- A8e3 (post-A8e2, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-3 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on packet staleness; no VETO): six narrow cleanup fixes. **H1** A8 packet § 3 A1 description: "Mathematically conservative" → "least-strict admissible value" with explicit Type-I implications. **H2** packet § 3 A6 description: 6 → 9 anchor sites (added F4 swarm sites explicitly). **H3** packet § 6 Q2.a / Q4.a / Q4.d marked CLOSED with closure rationale. **H4** `genesis_payload.toml` TR header phrasing about A1: "conservative ceiling" → "max-tolerated ceiling". **H5** § 5 item 7 here in TRACE_MATRIX: "(commit pending) + 6 wired" → "CLOSED + 9 wired". **H6** G1 wrapper test fail-closed on missing `python3` (was soft skip; Codex R3 caught the silent-pass risk); explicit opt-out `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` for deliberate downgrades (logged loudly). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hashes; no new files). Non-blocking observation deferred to Phase B+: `make_pput` signature was 21 positional args at round-3; A8e fix F1 added the `run_id` parameter, bringing the post-A8e count to **24** (Gemini R3 Finding 2 + Gemini R5/Codex R6 spot-checks; A8e6 K3 corrected here) — refactor to a builder pattern is recommended but not gating.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8681:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:5277:- A8e2 (post-A8e, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-2 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent): three dual-audit fixes. **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1) `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` wraps `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` in a Rust integration test, making the V3L-27 round-robin conformance gate run on every `cargo test --workspace`. **G2** (Codex R2#2) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8` audit-requirements paragraph reworded — round-1 fix corrected § 2 but left § 8 still calling the substitution a "strictest plausible bar"; now consistent across the doc. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **G3** (Codex R2#3 + Gemini R2#2) `A8_EXIT_PACKET` updated for round-2 reality: § 2 cumulative-tests table extended with A8e/A8e2 columns, § 4 manifest-clean assertion bumped 30→34, § 5 round-1 risk #5 (no FC1-N12 in swarm verify path) removed (closed by F4), § 6 Q7.a/b numbers updated, round-2 outcome + round-3 fixes section appended; TRACE_MATRIX stale `run_corr_id` symbol row replaced; "in CI" softened to "via Rust wrapper post-A8e2". FC-trace: meta governance + V3L-27 mitigation gating (FC1-N7). Trust Root manifest 33 → 34 (`tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs`).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8682:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:5278:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8716:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6168:    84	    let keys: Vec<&str> = entries.iter().map(|(k, _)| k.as_str()).collect();
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8717:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6295:    27	**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8718:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6299:    31	- The substitution may be LESS protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`. When calibration runs (per § 3 conditions), the empirical estimate likely tightens the bar; until then we operate at the ceiling.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8719:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6353:    85	"handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md" = "<sha256>"

(Should not — `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family, so no Type-I inflation. Per § 2 wording correction: 0.10 is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling, NOT a tighter-than-original substitute — the substitution may be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`, accepted because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first.)

Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hashes; no new files). Non-blocking observation deferred to Phase B+: `make_pput` signature was 21 positional args at round-3; A8e fix F1 added the `run_id` parameter, bringing the post-A8e count to **24** (Gemini R3 Finding 2 + Gemini R5/Codex R6 spot-checks; A8e6 K3 corrected here) — refactor to a builder pattern is recommended but not gating.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8723:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6380:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:18:- A8e2 (post-A8e, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-2 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent): three dual-audit fixes. **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1) `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` wraps `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` in a Rust integration test, making the V3L-27 round-robin conformance gate run on every `cargo test --workspace`. **G2** (Codex R2#2) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8` audit-requirements paragraph reworded — round-1 fix corrected § 2 but left § 8 still calling the substitution a "strictest plausible bar"; now consistent across the doc. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **G3** (Codex R2#3 + Gemini R2#2) `A8_EXIT_PACKET` updated for round-2 reality: § 2 cumulative-tests table extended with A8e/A8e2 columns, § 4 manifest-clean assertion bumped 30→34, § 5 round-1 risk #5 (no FC1-N12 in swarm verify path) removed (closed by F4), § 6 Q7.a/b numbers updated, round-2 outcome + round-3 fixes section appended; TRACE_MATRIX stale `run_corr_id` symbol row replaced; "in CI" softened to "via Rust wrapper post-A8e2". FC-trace: meta governance + V3L-27 mitigation gating (FC1-N7). Trust Root manifest 33 → 34 (`tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs`).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8724:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6381:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:19:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8725:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6383:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:67:- **Statistical framing** (post-A8e F6 + A8e2 G2 wording corrections): `0.10` is the **least-strict admissible value** the PREREG ceiling allows; `j-RR ≤ p_0` makes a SMALLER `p_0` stricter, so this substitution is operationally permitted but NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family. May be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`; acceptable because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8726:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6385:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:27:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8727:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6387:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:95:- Does substitution of `p_0 = 0.10` invalidate any Gate H statistical claim? (Should not — `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family, so no Type-I inflation. Per § 2 wording correction: 0.10 is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling, NOT a tighter-than-original substitute — the substitution may be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`, accepted because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first.)
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8728:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6393:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:67:- **Statistical framing** (post-A8e F6 + A8e2 G2 wording corrections): `0.10` is the **least-strict admissible value** the PREREG ceiling allows; `j-RR ≤ p_0` makes a SMALLER `p_0` stricter, so this substitution is operationally permitted but NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family. May be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`; acceptable because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8729:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6396:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:31:- The substitution may be LESS protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`. Explicit opt-out only via `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` and the bypass is logged loudly. Closes the round-2 "recurring conformance gate" finding.

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8959:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:184:- Substitutes `p_0 = 0.10` (PREREG § 5.5 ceiling) for the calibration-derived value at every Gate H consumer. The substitution is operationally permitted (the PREREG explicitly admits up to 0.10 as the ceiling) but is the **least-strict admissible value** since `j-RR ≤ p_0` makes a SMALLER `p_0` stricter — see PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 wording correction (round-1 audit Codex#1, A8e fix F6) for full statistical implications. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family. May be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`; acceptable because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first. Re-calibration conditions in § 3 list 5 items (N-experiments arc complete / swarm_N=1 mode landed / per-agent budget normalization landed / hetero-LLM exp complete / Phase D ArchitectAI runtime exists).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8960:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:189:- New `parse_swarm_condition_n` in `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs` discriminates `n<digits>` from `oneshot` / `hybrid_v1` / malformed. PREREG_AMENDMENT § 3 condition 2 cleared.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8961:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:212:- New module `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/fc_trace.rs`. Pure stdlib (zero new deps). 7-variant `FcId` enum (FC1-N7 / FC1-N11 / FC1-N12 / FC1-E18 / FC2-N20 / FC2-N22 / FC3-N31). `FC_TRACE=1` gate cached in `OnceLock`; `FC_TRACE_FILE=<path>` redirects emit to file.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8962:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:213:- 9 wired anchor sites total (round-1 had 6 — A8e fix F4 added 3 swarm verify sites): synthetic short-circuit / mr tick / OMEGA full-proof / OMEGA per-tactic / natural MaxTxExhausted (with budget_regime payload from A5) / oneshot FC1-N12 verify bracket / **swarm `verify_omega_detailed` × 2 paths (alone + tape+payload retry)** / **swarm `verify_partial`**.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8963:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:220:- `src/drivers/llm_proxy.py` ported from v3 with one load-bearing v4 change: per-provider multi-key round-robin. PROVIDERS map now holds a list of env names per provider; `get_client_round_robin` distributes via `_rr_counters` mod `len(clients)`. `/stats` exposes `per_key_requests` for observability. New `siliconflow:<model>` provider-prefix syntax.

Removed from this list.)
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8965:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:250:- (Q1.a) Each of A1–A7 was committed as one atomic commit with `FC-trace: <FC?-N?>` in the commit message, per memory `feedback_fc_first_problem_handling`. Verify by re-reading each commit message. Are any commits missing FC-trace, or claiming an FC node that doesn't match the actual code change?
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8966:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:334:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8967:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:338:- The substitution may be LESS protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`. When calibration runs (per § 3 conditions), the empirical estimate likely tightens the bar; until then we operate at the ceiling.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8968:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:392:"handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md" = "<sha256>"
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8969:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:402:- Does substitution of `p_0 = 0.10` invalidate any Gate H statistical claim? (Should not — `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family, so no Type-I inflation. Per § 2 wording correction: 0.10 is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling, NOT a tighter-than-original substitute — the substitution may be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`, accepted because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first.)

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6525:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:219:- Substitutes `p_0 = 0.10` (PREREG § 5.5 ceiling) for the calibration-derived value at every Gate H consumer. The substitution is operationally permitted (the PREREG explicitly admits up to 0.10 as the ceiling) but is the **least-strict admissible value** since `j-RR ≤ p_0` makes a SMALLER `p_0` stricter — see PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 wording correction (round-1 audit Codex#1, A8e fix F6) for full statistical implications. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family. May be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`; acceptable because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first. Re-calibration conditions in § 3 list 5 items (N-experiments arc complete / swarm_N=1 mode landed / per-agent budget normalization landed / hetero-LLM exp complete / Phase D ArchitectAI runtime exists).

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6535:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:261:- Trust Root manifest 28 → 31 (3 entries: `llm_proxy.py` + 2 smoke scripts).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6536:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:275:1. **`per_agent` budget regime untested at runtime**. A5 unit tests verify the scaling math (`base × N`) and env-coupled resolver. No live-LLM run with `BUDGET_REGIME=per_agent` has been smoked. Phase B kernel instrumentation will be the first opportunity to observe its behavior on a real problem; defer treatment to PREREG re-calibration if any anomaly surfaces.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6537:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:277:3. **SiliconFlow rate-limit at scale**. A7 verified 3 keys responding individually at N=1 concurrency. V3L-27 demonstrates collapse at N=30 single-key. The v4 multi-key round-robin should triple the safe N envelope but the actual sweet spot for our hetero swarm is unmeasured. Phase D heterogeneous-batch design should land a `--max-concurrency` knob (currently `LLM_PROXY_CONCURRENCY=5` env in proxy) tuned per provider.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6538:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:278:4. **Heterogeneous swarm = Phase D, not B/C**. Per F-2026-04-25-02 + the `agent_models.rs` `PHASE_D_HETERO_GATE_ENV_VAR` invariant, Phases B and C MUST stay single-model so ablation axes are not confounded. A7's plumbing exists for future Phase D work; Phase B uses the existing `deepseek-v4-flash` thinking-off backbone unchanged.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6539:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:285:- (Q1.a) Each of A1–A7 was committed as one atomic commit with `FC-trace: <FC?-N?>` in the commit message, per memory `feedback_fc_first_problem_handling`. Verify by re-reading each commit message. Are any commits missing FC-trace, or claiming an FC node that doesn't match the actual code change?
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6540:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:290:- (Q2.a) ~~Substituting `p_0 = 0.10` at the Gate H consumer (the PREREG § 5.5 ceiling) is described as conservative.~~ **CLOSED A8e F6 + A8e2 G2 + A8e3 H1**: the substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT conservative. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4). Wording fixed in PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 (A8e F6) + § 8 (A8e2 G2) + this packet's A1-atom description (A8e3 H1). No further verification needed.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6541:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:294:### Q3 — Budget regime soundness (A5)

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6543:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:303:- (Q4.d) ~~`run_corr_id` format = `condition_problem_id_unix-ms`. `make_pput`'s `run_id` independently re-computes this with its own ts. The two will differ by milliseconds. Is the join semantics for Phase D consumers documented anywhere?~~ **CLOSED A8e F1**: `run_corr_id` was renamed to `run_id`, lifted to `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs::mint_run_id`, and threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` so they stamp the same identifier (zero ms drift). Phase D joins by `run_id` equality. No further work.

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6546:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:313:~~6 new entries this Phase A: PREREG amendment (A1) + budget_regime.rs (A5) + fc_trace.rs (A6) + llm_proxy.py + smoke_siliconflow.sh + _smoke_siliconflow.py (A7).~~ **CORRECTED A8e3 H4 + A8e4 I2 + A8e5 J3**: 10 new entries this Phase A: PREREG amendment (A1) + agent_models.rs (A3 — was already in TR before A1; round-1 packet undercounted by 1) + budget_regime.rs (A5) + fc_trace.rs (A6) + llm_proxy.py + smoke_siliconflow.sh + _smoke_siliconflow.py (A7) + run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py (A8e) + llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs (A8e2). Final count 34.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6547:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:314:- (Q6.a) Are all 6 truly load-bearing? E.g., does tampering with `_smoke_siliconflow.py` actually weaken the constitutional gate, or is it a one-shot acceptance script?
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6548:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:315:- (Q6.b) `llm_proxy.py` is in Python — Trust Root verifies SHA-256, but does NOT verify Python interpreter behavior. A subtle algorithmic tamper (e.g., always-key-0 instead of round-robin) would change the SHA but a no-op cosmetic edit wouldn't trigger any acceptance test. Is the smoke script's `[2,2,2]` invariant codified anywhere as a recurring conformance test?
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6549:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:316:- (Q6.c) `.env` (containing live API keys) is gitignored and NOT in Trust Root. Should the absence of `.env` be a Trust Root constraint (boot fails if `.env` is missing OR if it's tracked)?
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6550:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:358:| Total batch wall-clock | ~8 hours | Realistic 3-7 days (576 runs × 15 min serial; treatment short-circuits halve some) | 9-21× |
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6551:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:369:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6552:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:388:3. **Per-agent budget normalization landed** (Phase A atom A5) — current `max_transactions=200` is fixed-tx budget; PREREG § 5.5 implicitly assumes tx-budget but doesn't specify; need explicit budget regime declaration for calibration to be reproducible.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6553:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:419:`PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md` itself is **NOT EDITED** by this amendment. It remains the immutable round-4 frozen pre-registration. This amendment is a separate document referenced from § 5.5 forward via a pointer added to Trust Root manifest.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6554:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:421:This pattern is per CLAUDE.md "Common Law": amendments are recorded as separate cases / docs that supersede specific sections, leaving the original frozen for reproducibility. PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md SHA-256 in Trust Root manifest UNCHANGED.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6555:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:430:Manifest size: 24 → 25 entries.

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6628:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:5139:- A5 (post-A4): per-agent budget normalization — new `budget_regime` module (`BUDGET_REGIME` + `MAX_TRANSACTIONS` env vars; 4-variant enum; pure parser + scaler + env-coupled resolver); `budget_regime` + `budget_max_transactions` added as non-Optional v2 fields on `RunAggregate` and the legacy `PputResult`; loop bound at `run_swarm` switched from hardcoded `let max_transactions = 200` to `resolve_budget(n_agents)` — default (env unset) preserves Phase B baseline (`total_proposal × 200`) bit-for-bit. PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer § 3 condition 3 satisfied: `MaxTxExhausted` rows now disambiguated across N values. FC-trace: FC2-N22 (HALT decomposition by budget regime) + FC1-N7 (δ instances determining the per-agent share under PerAgent regime). Trust Root manifest 26 → 27 (corrected A8e5 J4 — the A1 PREREG amendment had taken the count to 25, A3's `agent_models.rs` to 26 before A5 landed; round-1 packet undercounted by 1).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6629:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:5140:- A6 (post-A5): per-line FC tagging via structured JSON events — new `fc_trace` module (pure stdlib; zero new deps); `FcId` enum (FC1-N7 / FC1-N11 / FC1-N12 / FC1-E18 / FC2-N20 / FC2-N22 / FC3-N31); `fc_event!`-style `emit_event` API; `FC_TRACE=1` gate (cached in `OnceLock`); `FC_TRACE_FILE=<path>` redirects emit to file (default sink stderr). Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`): FC2-N22 synthetic short-circuit (swarm), FC2-N20 mr tick (swarm), FC2-N22 OMEGA full-proof (swarm), FC2-N22 OMEGA per-tactic (swarm), FC2-N22 natural MaxTxExhausted (swarm, with `budget_regime` payload), FC1-N12 verify bracket (oneshot). A8e fix F4 added 3 more in `run_swarm` (verify_omega_detailed × 2 + verify_partial), bringing the post-A8e total to 9 production emit sites. End-to-end smoke test exercises FC_TRACE=1 in a child process (subprocess required because `OnceLock` caches the gate-read; resolves item 7 of TRACE_MATRIX § 5 "Per-line FC tagging via tracing crate"). FC-trace: meta-witness for the 5-step compile loop (Proposal → Lean ground truth → Logging → Capability compilation → ↑H-VPPUT). Trust Root manifest 27 → 28 (corrected A8e5 J4; matches the canonical chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6630:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:5141:- A7 (post-A6): heterogeneous-LLM provider plumbing — `src/drivers/llm_proxy.py` ported from v3 with one load-bearing v4 change (per-provider multi-key round-robin: 3 SiliconFlow keys split concurrent traffic across separate rate-limit pools, mitigating V3L-27 single-key N=30 collapse). `scripts/smoke_siliconflow.sh` + `scripts/_smoke_siliconflow.py` probe each of the 3 keys (Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct, max_tokens=8) — A7 verified all 3 keys responding 2026-04-26 (1.5–3s latency, 33+1 tokens; round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls). New `siliconflow:<model>` provider-prefix syntax in `detect_provider()` for unambiguous routing in `AGENT_MODELS` payloads (Phase D heterogeneous swarms). Memory `reference_siliconflow.md` records SiliconFlow as the heterogeneous-LLM lane (NOT a fallback target). ./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6632:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:5143:- A8e4 (post-A8e3, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-4 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — split verdict, conservative merge wins per memory `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`): three narrow documentary defects Codex found in the packet itself. **I1** packet title + date: "round 2" → "running through rounds 1–N" (cumulative, with reader pointer to most-recent round section). **I2** per-atom Trust Root deltas in § 3: A5 25→26 → 26→27, A6 26→27 → 27→28, A7 27→30 → 28→31 (all corrected to match the chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header — round-1 packet undercounted because A3's `agent_models.rs` had already raised the count to 26 before A5 landed); TRACE_MATRIX § 1 line 11 anchor-site location: "Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`" → "Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`)" — same in § 5 item 7 closure note. **I3** `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` file header docstring: "if not, it skips with a clear diagnostic" → "if not, it FAILS CLOSED with a clear diagnostic" (A8e3 H6 fixed the runtime but left the docstring stale; Codex R4#3 caught the contradiction). ./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6633:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:5144:- A8e3 (post-A8e2, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-3 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on packet staleness; no VETO): six narrow cleanup fixes. **H1** A8 packet § 3 A1 description: "Mathematically conservative" → "least-strict admissible value" with explicit Type-I implications. **H2** packet § 3 A6 description: 6 → 9 anchor sites (added F4 swarm sites explicitly). **H3** packet § 6 Q2.a / Q4.a / Q4.d marked CLOSED with closure rationale. **H4** `genesis_payload.toml` TR header phrasing about A1: "conservative ceiling" → "max-tolerated ceiling". **H5** § 5 item 7 here in TRACE_MATRIX: "(commit pending) + 6 wired" → "CLOSED + 9 wired". **H6** G1 wrapper test fail-closed on missing `python3` (was soft skip; Codex R3 caught the silent-pass risk); explicit opt-out `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` for deliberate downgrades (logged loudly). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hashes; no new files). ./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6634:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:5145:- A8e2 (post-A8e, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-2 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent): three dual-audit fixes. **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1) `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` wraps `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` in a Rust integration test, making the V3L-27 round-robin conformance gate run on every `cargo test --workspace`. **G2** (Codex R2#2) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8` audit-requirements paragraph reworded — round-1 fix corrected § 2 but left § 8 still calling the substitution a "strictest plausible bar"; now consistent across the doc. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **G3** (Codex R2#3 + Gemini R2#2) `A8_EXIT_PACKET` updated for round-2 reality: § 2 cumulative-tests table extended with A8e/A8e2 columns, § 4 manifest-clean assertion bumped 30→34, § 5 round-1 risk #5 (no FC1-N12 in swarm verify path) removed (closed by F4), § 6 Q7.a/b numbers updated, round-2 outcome + round-3 fixes section appended; TRACE_MATRIX stale `run_corr_id` symbol row replaced; "in CI" softened to "via Rust wrapper post-A8e2". FC-trace: meta governance + V3L-27 mitigation gating (FC1-N7). Trust Root manifest 33 → 34 (`tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs`).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6635:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:5146:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

Anchor sites: FC2-N22 synthetic short-circuit / mr tick FC2-N20 / OMEGA full-proof FC2-N22 / OMEGA per-tactic FC2-N22 / natural MaxTxExhausted FC2-N22 (with `budget_regime` payload from A5) / FC1-N12 oneshot verify (A6) / FC1-N12 swarm `verify_omega_detailed` × 2 paths (A8e F4) / FC1-N12 swarm `verify_partial` (A8e F4). ./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6726:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:184:- Substitutes `p_0 = 0.10` (PREREG § 5.5 ceiling) for the calibration-derived value at every Gate H consumer. The substitution is operationally permitted (the PREREG explicitly admits up to 0.10 as the ceiling) but is the **least-strict admissible value** since `j-RR ≤ p_0` makes a SMALLER `p_0` stricter — see PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 wording correction (round-1 audit Codex#1, A8e fix F6) for full statistical implications. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family. May be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`; acceptable because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first. Re-calibration conditions in § 3 list 5 items (N-experiments arc complete / swarm_N=1 mode landed / per-agent budget normalization landed / hetero-LLM exp complete / Phase D ArchitectAI runtime exists).

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6736:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:226:- Trust Root manifest 27 → 30.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6737:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:240:1. **`per_agent` budget regime untested at runtime**. A5 unit tests verify the scaling math (`base × N`) and env-coupled resolver. No live-LLM run with `BUDGET_REGIME=per_agent` has been smoked. Phase B kernel instrumentation will be the first opportunity to observe its behavior on a real problem; defer treatment to PREREG re-calibration if any anomaly surfaces.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6738:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:242:3. **SiliconFlow rate-limit at scale**. A7 verified 3 keys responding individually at N=1 concurrency. V3L-27 demonstrates collapse at N=30 single-key. The v4 multi-key round-robin should triple the safe N envelope but the actual sweet spot for our hetero swarm is unmeasured. Phase D heterogeneous-batch design should land a `--max-concurrency` knob (currently `LLM_PROXY_CONCURRENCY=5` env in proxy) tuned per provider.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6739:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:243:4. **Heterogeneous swarm = Phase D, not B/C**. Per F-2026-04-25-02 + the `agent_models.rs` `PHASE_D_HETERO_GATE_ENV_VAR` invariant, Phases B and C MUST stay single-model so ablation axes are not confounded. A7's plumbing exists for future Phase D work; Phase B uses the existing `deepseek-v4-flash` thinking-off backbone unchanged.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6740:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:250:- (Q1.a) Each of A1–A7 was committed as one atomic commit with `FC-trace: <FC?-N?>` in the commit message, per memory `feedback_fc_first_problem_handling`. Verify by re-reading each commit message. Are any commits missing FC-trace, or claiming an FC node that doesn't match the actual code change?
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6741:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:255:- (Q2.a) ~~Substituting `p_0 = 0.10` at the Gate H consumer (the PREREG § 5.5 ceiling) is described as conservative.~~ **CLOSED A8e F6 + A8e2 G2 + A8e3 H1**: the substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT conservative. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4). Wording fixed in PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 (A8e F6) + § 8 (A8e2 G2) + this packet's A1-atom description (A8e3 H1). No further verification needed.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6742:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:259:### Q3 — Budget regime soundness (A5)
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6743:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:264:### Q4 — FC tracing coverage (A6)

Is the join semantics for Phase D consumers documented anywhere?~~ **CLOSED A8e F1**: `run_corr_id` was renamed to `run_id`, lifted to `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs::mint_run_id`, and threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` so they stamp the same identifier (zero ms drift). Phase D joins by `run_id` equality. No further work.

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6747:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:278:6 new entries this Phase A: PREREG amendment (A1) + budget_regime.rs (A5) + fc_trace.rs (A6) + llm_proxy.py + smoke_siliconflow.sh + _smoke_siliconflow.py (A7).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6748:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:279:- (Q6.a) Are all 6 truly load-bearing? E.g., does tampering with `_smoke_siliconflow.py` actually weaken the constitutional gate, or is it a one-shot acceptance script?
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6749:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:280:- (Q6.b) `llm_proxy.py` is in Python — Trust Root verifies SHA-256, but does NOT verify Python interpreter behavior. A subtle algorithmic tamper (e.g., always-key-0 instead of round-robin) would change the SHA but a no-op cosmetic edit wouldn't trigger any acceptance test. Is the smoke script's `[2,2,2]` invariant codified anywhere as a recurring conformance test?
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6750:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:281:- (Q6.c) `.env` (containing live API keys) is gitignored and NOT in Trust Root. ./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6751:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:323:| Total batch wall-clock | ~8 hours | Realistic 3-7 days (576 runs × 15 min serial; treatment short-circuits halve some) | 9-21× |
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6752:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:334:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6753:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:353:3. **Per-agent budget normalization landed** (Phase A atom A5) — current `max_transactions=200` is fixed-tx budget; PREREG § 5.5 implicitly assumes tx-budget but doesn't specify; need explicit budget regime declaration for calibration to be reproducible.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6754:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:384:`PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md` itself is **NOT EDITED** by this amendment. It remains the immutable round-4 frozen pre-registration. This amendment is a separate document referenced from § 5.5 forward via a pointer added to Trust Root manifest.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6755:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:386:This pattern is per CLAUDE.md "Common Law": amendments are recorded as separate cases / docs that supersede specific sections, leaving the original frozen for reproducibility. PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md SHA-256 in Trust Root manifest UNCHANGED.

Audit packet should specifically test:
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6758:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:402:- Does substitution of `p_0 = 0.10` invalidate any Gate H statistical claim? (Should not — `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family, so no Type-I inflation. Per § 2 wording correction: 0.10 is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling, NOT a tighter-than-original substitute — the substitution may be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`, accepted because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first.)

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6828:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5102:- A0d (this doc): Trust Root manifest 20 → 24 (this commit); v2 documents the harness as constitutional artifact
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6829:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5104:- A5 (post-A4): per-agent budget normalization — new `budget_regime` module (`BUDGET_REGIME` + `MAX_TRANSACTIONS` env vars; 4-variant enum; pure parser + scaler + env-coupled resolver); `budget_regime` + `budget_max_transactions` added as non-Optional v2 fields on `RunAggregate` and the legacy `PputResult`; loop bound at `run_swarm` switched from hardcoded `let max_transactions = 200` to `resolve_budget(n_agents)` — default (env unset) preserves Phase B baseline (`total_proposal × 200`) bit-for-bit. PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer § 3 condition 3 satisfied: `MaxTxExhausted` rows now disambiguated across N values. FC-trace: FC2-N22 (HALT decomposition by budget regime) + FC1-N7 (δ instances determining the per-agent share under PerAgent regime). Trust Root manifest 25 → 26.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6830:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5105:- A6 (post-A5): per-line FC tagging via structured JSON events — new `fc_trace` module (pure stdlib; zero new deps); `FcId` enum (FC1-N7 / FC1-N11 / FC1-N12 / FC1-E18 / FC2-N20 / FC2-N22 / FC3-N31); `fc_event!`-style `emit_event` API; `FC_TRACE=1` gate (cached in `OnceLock`); `FC_TRACE_FILE=<path>` redirects emit to file (default sink stderr). Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`: FC2-N22 synthetic short-circuit, FC2-N20 mr tick, FC2-N22 OMEGA full-proof, FC2-N22 OMEGA per-tactic, FC2-N22 natural MaxTxExhausted (with `budget_regime` payload), FC1-N12 verify bracket (oneshot). End-to-end smoke test exercises FC_TRACE=1 in a child process (subprocess required because `OnceLock` caches the gate-read; resolves item 7 of TRACE_MATRIX § 5 "Per-line FC tagging via tracing crate"). FC-trace: meta-witness for the 5-step compile loop (Proposal → Lean ground truth → Logging → Capability compilation → ↑H-VPPUT). Trust Root manifest 26 → 27.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6831:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5106:- A7 (post-A6): heterogeneous-LLM provider plumbing — `src/drivers/llm_proxy.py` ported from v3 with one load-bearing v4 change (per-provider multi-key round-robin: 3 SiliconFlow keys split concurrent traffic across separate rate-limit pools, mitigating V3L-27 single-key N=30 collapse). `scripts/smoke_siliconflow.sh` + `scripts/_smoke_siliconflow.py` probe each of the 3 keys (Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct, max_tokens=8) — A7 verified all 3 keys responding 2026-04-26 (1.5–3s latency, 33+1 tokens; round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls). New `siliconflow:<model>` provider-prefix syntax in `detect_provider()` for unambiguous routing in `AGENT_MODELS` payloads (Phase D heterogeneous swarms). Memory `reference_siliconflow.md` records SiliconFlow as the heterogeneous-LLM lane (NOT a fallback target). FC-trace: FC1-N7 (δ/AI provider expansion — heterogeneous δ instances across SF catalog enable Phase D meta-loop). Trust Root manifest 28 → 31 (proxy + 2 smoke scripts).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6832:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5107:- A8e3 (post-A8e2, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-3 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on packet staleness; no VETO): six narrow cleanup fixes. **H1** A8 packet § 3 A1 description: "Mathematically conservative" → "least-strict admissible value" with explicit Type-I implications. **H2** packet § 3 A6 description: 6 → 9 anchor sites (added F4 swarm sites explicitly). **H3** packet § 6 Q2.a / Q4.a / Q4.d marked CLOSED with closure rationale. **H4** `genesis_payload.toml` TR header phrasing about A1: "conservative ceiling" → "max-tolerated ceiling". **H5** § 5 item 7 here in TRACE_MATRIX: "(commit pending) + 6 wired" → "CLOSED + 9 wired". **H6** G1 wrapper test fail-closed on missing `python3` (was soft skip; Codex R3 caught the silent-pass risk); explicit opt-out `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` for deliberate downgrades (logged loudly). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hashes; no new files). Non-blocking observation deferred to Phase B+: `make_pput` signature is now 21 positional args (Gemini R3 Finding 2) — refactor to a builder pattern is recommended but not gating.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6833:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5108:- A8e2 (post-A8e, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-2 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent): three dual-audit fixes. **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1) `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` wraps `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` in a Rust integration test, making the V3L-27 round-robin conformance gate run on every `cargo test --workspace`. **G2** (Codex R2#2) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8` audit-requirements paragraph reworded — round-1 fix corrected § 2 but left § 8 still calling the substitution a "strictest plausible bar"; now consistent across the doc. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **G3** (Codex R2#3 + Gemini R2#2) `A8_EXIT_PACKET` updated for round-2 reality: § 2 cumulative-tests table extended with A8e/A8e2 columns, § 4 manifest-clean assertion bumped 30→34, § 5 round-1 risk #5 (no FC1-N12 in swarm verify path) removed (closed by F4), § 6 Q7.a/b numbers updated, round-2 outcome + round-3 fixes section appended; TRACE_MATRIX stale `run_corr_id` symbol row replaced; "in CI" softened to "via Rust wrapper post-A8e2". FC-trace: meta governance + V3L-27 mitigation gating (FC1-N7). Trust Root manifest 33 → 34 (`tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs`).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6834:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5109:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6877:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5406:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:11:- A6 (post-A5): per-line FC tagging via structured JSON events — new `fc_trace` module (pure stdlib; zero new deps); `FcId` enum (FC1-N7 / FC1-N11 / FC1-N12 / FC1-E18 / FC2-N20 / FC2-N22 / FC3-N31); `fc_event!`-style `emit_event` API; `FC_TRACE=1` gate (cached in `OnceLock`); `FC_TRACE_FILE=<path>` redirects emit to file (default sink stderr). Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`: FC2-N22 synthetic short-circuit, FC2-N20 mr tick, FC2-N22 OMEGA full-proof, FC2-N22 OMEGA per-tactic, FC2-N22 natural MaxTxExhausted (with `budget_regime` payload), FC1-N12 verify bracket (oneshot). End-to-end smoke test exercises FC_TRACE=1 in a child process (subprocess required because `OnceLock` caches the gate-read; resolves item 7 of TRACE_MATRIX § 5 "Per-line FC tagging via tracing crate"). FC-trace: meta-witness for the 5-step compile loop (Proposal → Lean ground truth → Logging → Capability compilation → ↑H-VPPUT). Trust Root manifest 26 → 27.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6878:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5407:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:13:- A8e3 (post-A8e2, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-3 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on packet staleness; no VETO): six narrow cleanup fixes. **H1** A8 packet § 3 A1 description: "Mathematically conservative" → "least-strict admissible value" with explicit Type-I implications. **H2** packet § 3 A6 description: 6 → 9 anchor sites (added F4 swarm sites explicitly). **H3** packet § 6 Q2.a / Q4.a / Q4.d marked CLOSED with closure rationale. **H4** `genesis_payload.toml` TR header phrasing about A1: "conservative ceiling" → "max-tolerated ceiling". **H5** § 5 item 7 here in TRACE_MATRIX: "(commit pending) + 6 wired" → "CLOSED + 9 wired". **H6** G1 wrapper test fail-closed on missing `python3` (was soft skip; Codex R3 caught the silent-pass risk); explicit opt-out `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` for deliberate downgrades (logged loudly). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hashes; no new files). Non-blocking observation deferred to Phase B+: `make_pput` signature is now 21 positional args (Gemini R3 Finding 2) — refactor to a builder pattern is recommended but not gating.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6879:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5408:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:14:- A8e2 (post-A8e, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-2 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent): three dual-audit fixes. **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1) `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` wraps `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` in a Rust integration test, making the V3L-27 round-robin conformance gate run on every `cargo test --workspace`. **G2** (Codex R2#2) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8` audit-requirements paragraph reworded — round-1 fix corrected § 2 but left § 8 still calling the substitution a "strictest plausible bar"; now consistent across the doc. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **G3** (Codex R2#3 + Gemini R2#2) `A8_EXIT_PACKET` updated for round-2 reality: § 2 cumulative-tests table extended with A8e/A8e2 columns, § 4 manifest-clean assertion bumped 30→34, § 5 round-1 risk #5 (no FC1-N12 in swarm verify path) removed (closed by F4), § 6 Q7.a/b numbers updated, round-2 outcome + round-3 fixes section appended; TRACE_MATRIX stale `run_corr_id` symbol row replaced; "in CI" softened to "via Rust wrapper post-A8e2". FC-trace: meta governance + V3L-27 mitigation gating (FC1-N7). Trust Root manifest 33 → 34 (`tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs`).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6880:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5409:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:15:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

End-to-end smoke `tests/fc_trace_smoke.rs` exercises FC_TRACE=1 in a child process (forced because `OnceLock` caches the gate-read). | ✅ |
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6883:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5414:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:101:7. ~~**Per-line FC tagging via tracing crate** — Plan agent's recommendation in N-experiments brainstorm. Phase A6 deferred; will land before Phase B (homogeneous experiments).~~ **CLOSED**: A6 (`89994c7`) shipped `fc_trace.rs` + 7 anchor sites; A8e fix F4 added 2 swarm-side `verify_omega_detailed` paths + 1 `verify_partial`, bringing the total to **9 wired anchor sites**. Implementation chose pure stdlib over the `tracing` crate to avoid a new dep tree; the macro surface (`emit_event` + `FcId` enum) was kept small so Phase D+ can swap to a real `tracing-subscriber` bridge locally.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6884:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5416:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:27:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6885:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5417:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:92:Per case C-073 ArchitectAI commit workflow: this amendment requires dual audit (Codex + Gemini, conservative VETO > CHALLENGE > PASS) before commit lands. Audit packet should specifically test:

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6929:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5621:    10	- A5 (post-A4): per-agent budget normalization — new `budget_regime` module (`BUDGET_REGIME` + `MAX_TRANSACTIONS` env vars; 4-variant enum; pure parser + scaler + env-coupled resolver); `budget_regime` + `budget_max_transactions` added as non-Optional v2 fields on `RunAggregate` and the legacy `PputResult`; loop bound at `run_swarm` switched from hardcoded `let max_transactions = 200` to `resolve_budget(n_agents)` — default (env unset) preserves Phase B baseline (`total_proposal × 200`) bit-for-bit. ./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6930:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5622:    11	- A6 (post-A5): per-line FC tagging via structured JSON events — new `fc_trace` module (pure stdlib; zero new deps); `FcId` enum (FC1-N7 / FC1-N11 / FC1-N12 / FC1-E18 / FC2-N20 / FC2-N22 / FC3-N31); `fc_event!`-style `emit_event` API; `FC_TRACE=1` gate (cached in `OnceLock`); `FC_TRACE_FILE=<path>` redirects emit to file (default sink stderr). Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`: FC2-N22 synthetic short-circuit, FC2-N20 mr tick, FC2-N22 OMEGA full-proof, FC2-N22 OMEGA per-tactic, FC2-N22 natural MaxTxExhausted (with `budget_regime` payload), FC1-N12 verify bracket (oneshot). End-to-end smoke test exercises FC_TRACE=1 in a child process (subprocess required because `OnceLock` caches the gate-read; resolves item 7 of TRACE_MATRIX § 5 "Per-line FC tagging via tracing crate"). FC-trace: meta-witness for the 5-step compile loop (Proposal → Lean ground truth → Logging → Capability compilation → ↑H-VPPUT). Trust Root manifest 26 → 27.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6931:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5623:    12	- A7 (post-A6): heterogeneous-LLM provider plumbing — `src/drivers/llm_proxy.py` ported from v3 with one load-bearing v4 change (per-provider multi-key round-robin: 3 SiliconFlow keys split concurrent traffic across separate rate-limit pools, mitigating V3L-27 single-key N=30 collapse). `scripts/smoke_siliconflow.sh` + `scripts/_smoke_siliconflow.py` probe each of the 3 keys (Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct, max_tokens=8) — A7 verified all 3 keys responding 2026-04-26 (1.5–3s latency, 33+1 tokens; round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls). New `siliconflow:<model>` provider-prefix syntax in `detect_provider()` for unambiguous routing in `AGENT_MODELS` payloads (Phase D heterogeneous swarms). Memory `reference_siliconflow.md` records SiliconFlow as the heterogeneous-LLM lane (NOT a fallback target). FC-trace: FC1-N7 (δ/AI provider expansion — heterogeneous δ instances across SF catalog enable Phase D meta-loop). Trust Root manifest 28 → 31 (proxy + 2 smoke scripts).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6932:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5624:    13	- A8e3 (post-A8e2, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-3 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on packet staleness; no VETO): six narrow cleanup fixes. **H1** A8 packet § 3 A1 description: "Mathematically conservative" → "least-strict admissible value" with explicit Type-I implications. **H2** packet § 3 A6 description: 6 → 9 anchor sites (added F4 swarm sites explicitly). **H3** packet § 6 Q2.a / Q4.a / Q4.d marked CLOSED with closure rationale. **H4** `genesis_payload.toml` TR header phrasing about A1: "conservative ceiling" → "max-tolerated ceiling". **H5** § 5 item 7 here in TRACE_MATRIX: "(commit pending) + 6 wired" → "CLOSED + 9 wired". **H6** G1 wrapper test fail-closed on missing `python3` (was soft skip; Codex R3 caught the silent-pass risk); explicit opt-out `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` for deliberate downgrades (logged loudly). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hashes; no new files). Non-blocking observation deferred to Phase B+: `make_pput` signature is now 21 positional args (Gemini R3 Finding 2) — refactor to a builder pattern is recommended but not gating.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6933:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5625:    14	- A8e2 (post-A8e, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-2 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent): three dual-audit fixes. **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1) `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` wraps `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` in a Rust integration test, making the V3L-27 round-robin conformance gate run on every `cargo test --workspace`. **G2** (Codex R2#2) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8` audit-requirements paragraph reworded — round-1 fix corrected § 2 but left § 8 still calling the substitution a "strictest plausible bar"; now consistent across the doc. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **G3** (Codex R2#3 + Gemini R2#2) `A8_EXIT_PACKET` updated for round-2 reality: § 2 cumulative-tests table extended with A8e/A8e2 columns, § 4 manifest-clean assertion bumped 30→34, § 5 round-1 risk #5 (no FC1-N12 in swarm verify path) removed (closed by F4), § 6 Q7.a/b numbers updated, round-2 outcome + round-3 fixes section appended; TRACE_MATRIX stale `run_corr_id` symbol row replaced; "in CI" softened to "via Rust wrapper post-A8e2". FC-trace: meta governance + V3L-27 mitigation gating (FC1-N7). Trust Root manifest 33 → 34 (`tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs`).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6934:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5626:    15	- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

Phase A scope = first two regimes implemented; latter two declared startup-fatal `UnimplementedRegime` so a misconfigured run aborts before consuming LLM budget. PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer § 3 condition 3 dependency cleared. | ✅ |
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6939:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5672:    61	| `RunAggregate::{budget_regime, budget_max_transactions}` + `PputResult::{budget_regime, budget_max_transactions}` (A5) | `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/jsonl_schema.rs` + `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs` | FC2-N22: every emitted v2 row stamps the regime label + base budget so downstream PPUT analysis can join on the partitioning rule. Loop bound at `run_swarm` startup = `resolve_budget(n_agents).effective_max_tx`; default (env unset) preserves the Phase B baseline `total_proposal × 200` bit-for-bit. 16 unit tests (15 in `budget_regime::tests` + 1 `test_a5_budget_regime_round_trip` in jsonl_schema). | ✅ |
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6940:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5673:    62	| `fc_trace::{FcId, FC_TRACE_*ENV*, fc_trace_enabled, emit_event, json_str}` (A6) | `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/fc_trace.rs` | meta-witness for FC1 / FC2 / FC3 path coverage. 7-variant `FcId` enum produces stable strings (`FC1-N7` / `FC1-N11` / `FC1-N12` / `FC1-E18` / `FC2-N20` / `FC2-N22` / `FC3-N31`) that Phase D consumers + TRACE_MATRIX rows join on. `FC_TRACE=1` gate cached in `OnceLock` (zero-overhead in production). 6 unit tests (label stability + JSON escape + cold-path no-op). | ✅ |
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6941:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5674:    63	| `run_id::mint_run_id` correlation key + 9 wired FC events (A6 + A8e F4) | `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` + `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs` | A6 introduced a per-run correlation id (originally named `run_corr_id`, now unified into `run_id`) anchoring all events. A8e fix F1 (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) split this out into a dedicated module + threaded the same id into `make_pput` so FC events and v2 jsonl rows share one identifier (no ms drift). Anchor sites: FC2-N22 synthetic short-circuit / mr tick FC2-N20 / OMEGA full-proof FC2-N22 / OMEGA per-tactic FC2-N22 / natural MaxTxExhausted FC2-N22 (with `budget_regime` payload from A5) / FC1-N12 oneshot verify (A6) / FC1-N12 swarm `verify_omega_detailed` × 2 paths (A8e F4) / FC1-N12 swarm `verify_partial` (A8e F4). End-to-end smoke `tests/fc_trace_smoke.rs` exercises FC_TRACE=1 in a child process (forced because `OnceLock` caches the gate-read). | ✅ |

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6995:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5884:   144	- Substitutes `p_0 = 0.10` (PREREG § 5.5 ceiling) for the calibration-derived value at every Gate H consumer. The substitution is operationally permitted (the PREREG explicitly admits up to 0.10 as the ceiling) but is the **least-strict admissible value** since `j-RR ≤ p_0` makes a SMALLER `p_0` stricter — see PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 wording correction (round-1 audit Codex#1, A8e fix F6) for full statistical implications. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family. May be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`; acceptable because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first. Re-calibration conditions in § 3 list 5 items (N-experiments arc complete / swarm_N=1 mode landed / per-agent budget normalization landed / hetero-LLM exp complete / Phase D ArchitectAI runtime exists).

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:7071:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:6380:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:215:- (Q2.a) ~~Substituting `p_0 = 0.10` at the Gate H consumer (the PREREG § 5.5 ceiling) is described as conservative.~~ **CLOSED A8e F6 + A8e2 G2 + A8e3 H1**: the substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT conservative. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4). Wording fixed in PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 (A8e F6) + § 8 (A8e2 G2) + this packet's A1-atom description (A8e3 H1). No further verification needed.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:7072:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:6381:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:27:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:7145:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:7657:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:10:- A5 (post-A4): per-agent budget normalization — new `budget_regime` module (`BUDGET_REGIME` + `MAX_TRANSACTIONS` env vars; 4-variant enum; pure parser + scaler + env-coupled resolver); `budget_regime` + `budget_max_transactions` added as non-Optional v2 fields on `RunAggregate` and the legacy `PputResult`; loop bound at `run_swarm` switched from hardcoded `let max_transactions = 200` to `resolve_budget(n_agents)` — default (env unset) preserves Phase B baseline (`total_proposal × 200`) bit-for-bit. PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer § 3 condition 3 satisfied: `MaxTxExhausted` rows now disambiguated across N values.
